title: micropholis suborbicularis
intro: Micropholis suborbicularis, also known as the "Guatemalan forest tree," is a species of plant native to the tropical regions of Central America, characterized by its broad, rounded leaves and distinctive trunk. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which are attributed to its rich content of flavonoids and terpenoids. The health benefits of Micropholis suborbicularis include reducing inflammation and fighting off infections, as well as exhibiting antioxidant and cardiovascular protective properties. Herbal preparations of Micropholis suborbicularis are typically made from its leaves or bark, which are often used in teas, infusions, or as a topical cream to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
